document.title: Clinical Evaluation Plan for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Manufacturer clinical evaluation plan for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW under Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports the clinical evaluation and technical documentation by defining intended purpose, clinical benefits, evidence sources, equivalence approach, PMS/PMCF inputs, benefit-risk parameters, and applicable standards.
keyPoints: The document is A-TCF-5-117, Rev. No. 1, with revision date October 20, 2023.
The device is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children, excluding infants and pregnant women.
Clinical benefits are reduction in patient dose with high quality image and fast image acquisition time.
Clinical outcome parameters include DQE, MTF, recovery time, and image acquisition time.
The plan uses literature, equivalence data, PMS/PMCF, complaints, recall and vigilance data, and imaging evaluation as clinical evidence sources.
The document states there is no common specification for the device.

evidence.claim: The document is a clinical evaluation plan for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical Evaluation Plan for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The document number is A-TCF-5-117 and revision number is 1.
evidence.evidenceText: Technical Documentation Document No. A-TCF-5-117 Rev. No. 1

evidence.claim: The revision date is October 20, 2023.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical Evaluation Plan Rev. Date October 20, 2023

evidence.claim: Revision 1 was a response to first review by 3EC notified body.
evidence.evidenceText: 1 October 20, 2023 Response to 1st review by 3EC (NB)

evidence.claim: The product models covered are A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Model name: A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Models A1717MCW A1417MCW F1417MCW

evidence.claim: The plan is documented according to Part A of Annex XIV of Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
evidence.evidenceText: This clinical evaluation plan is documented according to the Part A of Annex XIV of Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
evidence.pageTarget: EU MDR Annex XIV Clinical Evaluation

evidence.claim: The device is indicated for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector (model: A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW) is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Intended Purpose

evidence.claim: The device is intended to replace film-based radiographic diagnostic systems.
evidence.evidenceText: It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide more precise case diagnosis for physicians and radiologists.

evidence.claim: The intended target groups are adults and children except infants and pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Adult and children except infant and pregnant women.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Intended Population

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography, infants and pregnant women, fluoroscopy, or angiography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women. Not to be used for fluoroscopy and angiography.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Contraindications

evidence.claim: The device's clinical benefits include reduction in patient dose with high quality image and fast image acquisition time.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical benefit 1 – Reduction in patient dose and high quality image. Clinical benefit 2 – Fast image acquisition time
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Clinical Benefits

evidence.claim: Clinical outcome parameters include DQE, MTF, recovery time, and image acquisition time.
evidence.evidenceText: Relevant clinical outcome parameters - Detective Quantum Efficiency (DQE) - Modulation Transfer Function (MTF) ... Relevant clinical outcome parameters - Recovery time - Image acquisition time
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Clinical Outcome Parameters

evidence.claim: The device under evaluation is an indirect energy conversion detector.
evidence.evidenceText: These proposed devices are the detectors with indirect energy conversion.

evidence.claim: The benchmark device is ADD, a previous generation of the device.
evidence.evidenceText: The benchmark device, which is previous generations of the device of A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW, is ADD.
evidence.pageTarget: ADD Digital Flat Panel X Ray Detector

evidence.claim: The benchmark ADD device is identified as Class IIa.
evidence.evidenceText: 4 Risk class Class IIa
evidence.pageTarget: ADD Digital Flat Panel X Ray Detector

evidence.claim: The proposed device and benchmark acquire X-ray images using a TFT panel.
evidence.evidenceText: Similarity with the device in question Acquire X-ray images using TFT panel.

evidence.claim: The device has no components including pharmaceutical, non-viable animal, or human tissues.
evidence.evidenceText: The Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector (model: A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW) doesn’t have components which include pharmaceutical, non-viable animal or human tissues.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Materials

evidence.claim: Literature searches use scientific literature databases such as PubMed.
evidence.evidenceText: The source of data will be from scientific literature databases (such as PubMed).

evidence.claim: Recall searches include FDA Recall, MFDS Product recall and Suspension of Sales, and MHRA Alerts and recalls.
evidence.evidenceText: Another the source of data will be from internet searches for recall data (such as FDA Recall, MFDS Product recall and Suspension of Sales, and MHRA Alerts and recalls)

evidence.claim: PMCF customer survey data and clinical evaluation data will be collected, analyzed, and reported every two years.
evidence.evidenceText: According to the PMCF plan (A-PMCFP-5-001 PMCF plan), customer survey data and clinical evaluation data will be collected, analyzed and reported every two years.

evidence.claim: The plan states there is no known side effect, but potential risks include physical damage.
evidence.evidenceText: There is no known side effect but there are potential risks such as physical damage.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Residual Risk

evidence.claim: The device is not subject to clinical investigation according to this plan.
evidence.evidenceText: Our device is not subject to Clinical investigation. Please refer to section 9 of this documentation.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Clinical Investigation

evidence.claim: There is no common specification for the device.
evidence.evidenceText: There is no common specification for the device.

evidence.claim: The clinical evaluation report update frequency is every two years.
evidence.evidenceText: The frequency of updates for the clinical evaluation report is every two years.

evidence.claim: The document lists EN 62220-1-1:2015 for determination of detective quantum efficiency for detectors used in radiographic imaging.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 62220-1-1:2015 Medical electrical equipment - Characteristics of digital X-ray imaging devices - Part 1-1: Determination of the detective quantum efficiency - Detectors used in radiographic imaging

reviewFlags.reason: Authority metadata is inferred from the document's MDR references and source authoritySignal; the document itself is manufacturer technical documentation, not an issued regulatory authority decision.
The next clinical evaluation section gives an update frequency of every two years but does not provide a specific next evaluation date.
Some source text appears to contain OCR or drafting errors, including repeated words and misspellings such as 'and and alternative treatment options' and 'commuted Radiography'.
Potential internal inconsistency in the standards table: EN ISO 10993-10:2010 is paired with title text referencing ISO 10993-10:2021.
